name: Lozman v. Riviera Beach
question: Is a floating structure a "vessel" under 1 U.S.C. § 3, thus triggering federal maritime jurisdiction, if that structure is indefinitely moored, receives power and other utilities from shore, and is not intended to be used in maritime transportation or commerce?
description: A case in which the Court held that the definition of vessel under 1 U.S.C. §3 is too broad and not any floating structure is considered a vessel.
